features.summary: Occidental reported record Q2 2022 free cash flow of $4.2 billion and completed its $5 billion debt reduction goal ahead of schedule, having repaid over $8 billion in debt year-to-date. The company initiated its $3 billion share repurchase program and has already bought back $1.1 billion in shares. Production from continuing operations was approximately 1.1 million BOE per day, in line with guidance. OxyChem delivered record earnings for the fourth consecutive quarter with $800 million EBIT, benefiting from strong pricing in caustic, chlorine and PVC markets.
features.management_outlook: Management expects to complete the $3 billion share repurchase program and reduce gross debt to the high teens by year-end 2022. For 2023, they plan to implement a sustainable common dividend at $40 WTI alongside continued share repurchases. The company announced a major $1.1 billion modernization project at the Battleground chemical plant over three years starting in 2023, expected to increase cash flow through improved margins and higher volumes. Production in the second half of 2022 is expected to average approximately 1.2 million BOE per day, notably higher than the first half.
features.risks: The company faces continued inflationary pressures, particularly in the Permian Basin, with cost increases of 7-10% versus original plans, though they've mitigated about 4% through operational savings. Third-party operational issues including downstream gas processing interruptions and unplanned disruptions have impacted production guidance. OxyChem expects softer second-half performance due to macroeconomic headwinds including interest rates, housing starts, and potential recession impacts, though conditions remain strong by historical standards.
features.key_drivers: Key production growth drivers include the successful Horn Mountain West tieback adding 30,000 bpd, expanded partnership with EcoPetrol in both Midland and Delaware basins with capital carry benefits, and a new 25-year production sharing agreement in Algeria adding 100 million barrels of reserves. The Inflation Reduction Act provides significant benefits including enhanced 45Q tax credits for carbon capture, reinstatement of Gulf of Mexico Lease Sale 257, and support for low carbon ventures development, which management views as net positive for the company.
